questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Soaps are effective for cleaning purposes because they can form micelles around oil droplets.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Soaps are sodium and potassium salts of higher fatty acids, which have both hydrophobic and hydrophilic parts.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Soaps can form micelles due to their amphipathic nature, which is correctly explained by the reason.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Synthetic detergents are more effective than soaps in hard water.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Synthetic detergents do not form insoluble salts with calcium and magnesium ions in hard water.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Synthetic detergents are more effective in hard water because they do not precipitate out as insoluble salts with calcium and magnesium ions.
